{"tool": "read_file", "status": "ok", "content": "200:     output [NumHarts-1:0][1:0]          irq_o         // level sensitive IR lines, mip & sip (async)
201: );
202: 
203:   localparam int unsigned AxiIdWidth    =  1;
204:   localparam int unsigned AxiAddrWidth  = 64;
205:   localparam int unsigned AxiDataWidth  = 64;
206:   localparam int unsigned AxiUserWidth  =  1;
207: 
208: 
209: 
210:   parameter  FUSE_MEM_SIZE = 150; // change this size when ever no of entries in FUSE mem is changed
211:   parameter NB_SLAVE = 1;
212:   parameter NB_PERIPHERALS = 14;
213: 
214:   logic [8*NB_PERIPHERALS-1:0] reglk_ctrl; 
215:   logic [4*NB_PERIPHERALS-1 :0]   acc_ctrl; // Access control values
216:   logic [3:0][NB_PERIPHERALS-1:0] acc_ctrl_c; 
217: 
218:    genvar i, j;
219:    generate
220:        for (i=0; i < 4; i++) begin
221:         for (j=0; j < NB_PERIPHERALS; j++) begin
222:            assign acc_ctrl_c[i][j] = acc_ctrl[j*4+i] | (j==5 && acc_ctrl[4*4+i]);
223:         end
224:        end 
225:    endgenerate 
226: 
227: 
228: 
229:   /////////////////////////////
230:   // Debug module and JTAG
231:   /////////////////////////////
232: 
233:   logic          debug_req_valid;
234:   logic          debug_req_ready;
235:   logic          debug_resp_valid;
236:   logic          debug_resp_ready;
237: 
238:   logic [255:0]  jtag_hash, ikey_hash, okey_hash;
239:   logic           jtag_unlock; 
240: 
241:   dm::dmi_req_t  debug_req;
242:   dm::dmi_resp_t debug_resp;
243: 
244: `ifdef RISCV_FESVR_SIM
245: 
246:   initial begin
247:     $display("[INFO] instantiating FESVR DTM in simulation.");
248:   end
249: 
250:   // SiFive's SimDTM Module
251:   // Converts to DPI calls
252:   logic [31:0] sim_exit; // TODO: wire this up in the testbench
253:   logic [1:0] debug_req_bits_op;
254:   assign dmi_req.op = dm::dtm_op_t'(debug_req_bits_op);
255: 
256:   SimDTM i_SimDTM (
257:     .clk                  ( clk_i                ),
258:     .reset                ( ~rst_ni              ),
259:     .debug_req_valid      ( debug_req_valid      ),
260:     .debug_req_ready      ( debug_req_ready      ),
261:     .debug_req_bits_addr  ( debug_req.addr       ),
262:     .debug_req_bits_op    ( debug_req_bits_op    ),
263:     .debug_req_bits_data  ( debug_req.data       ),
264:     .debug_resp_valid     ( debug_resp_valid     ),
265:     .debug_resp_ready     ( debug_resp_ready     ),
266:     .debug_resp_bits_resp ( debug_resp.resp      ),
267:     .debug_resp_bits_data ( debug_resp.data      ),
268:     .exit                 ( sim_exit             )
269:   );
270: 
271: `else // RISCV_FESVR_SIM
272: 
273:   logic        tck, tms, trst_n, tdi, tdo, tdo_oe;
274: 
275:   dmi_jtag i_dmi_jtag (
276:     .clk_i                                ,
277:     .rst_ni                               ,
278:     .testmode_i                           ,
279:     .we_flag          ( we_flag_4        ),
280:     .jtag_hash_i      ( jtag_hash        ),
281:     .okey_hash_i      ( okey_hash        ),
282:     .ikey_hash_i      ( ikey_hash        ),
283:     .jtag_unlock_o    ( jtag_unlock      ),
284:     .dmi_req_o        ( debug_req        ),
285:     .dmi_req_valid_o  ( debug_req_valid  ),
286:     .dmi_req_ready_i  ( debug_req_ready  ),
287:     .dmi_resp_i       ( debug_resp       ),
288:     .dmi_resp_ready_o ( debug_resp_ready ),
289:     .dmi_resp_valid_i ( debug_resp_valid ),
290:     .dmi_rst_no       (                  ), // not connected
291:     .tck_i            ( tck              ),
292:     .tms_i            ( tms              ),
293:     .trst_ni          ( trst_n           ),
294:     .td_i             ( tdi              ),
295:     .td_o             ( tdo              ),
296:     .tdo_oe_o         ( tdo_oe           )
297:   );
298: 
299: `ifdef RISCV_JTAG_SIM
300: 
301:   initial begin
302:     $display("[INFO] instantiating JTAG DTM in simul
[TRUNCATED]

900:   
901:   // reset controller logic
902:   logic rst_1;
903:   logic rst_2;
904:   logic rst_3;
905:   logic rst_4;
906:   logic rst_5;
907:   logic rst_6;

924:     .rst_ni                  ,
925:     .reglk_ctrl_i  ( reglk_ctrl[12*8+7:12*8]),
926:     .acct_ctrl_i   ( acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][12]),
927:     .debug_mode_i  ( debug_mode_i  ),
928:     .axi_req_i     ( rst_axi_req  ),
929:     .axi_resp_o    ( rst_axi_resp ),
930:     .rst_1,
931:     .rst_2,
932:     .rst_3,
933:     .rst_4,
934:     .rst_5,
935:     .rst_6,
936:     .rst_7,
937:     .rst_8,
938:     .rst_9,
939:     .rst_10,
940:     .rst_11,
941:     .rst_13
942:   );
943: 
944:   noc_axilite_bridge #(
945:     .SLAVE_RESP_BYTEWIDTH   ( 8             ),
946:     .SWAP_ENDIANESS         ( SwapEndianess )
947:   ) i_rst_axilite_bridge (
948:     .clk                    ( clk_i                         ),
949:     .rst                    ( ~rst_ni                       ),
950:     // to/from NOC
951:     .splitter_bridge_val    ( buf_ariane_rst_noc2_valid_i ),
952:     .splitter_bridge_data   ( buf_ariane_rst_noc2_data_i  ),
953:     .bridge_splitter_rdy    ( ariane_rst_buf_noc2_ready_o ),
954:     .bridge_splitter_val    ( ariane_rst_buf_noc3_valid_o ),
955:     .bridge_splitter_data   ( ariane_rst_buf_noc3_data_o  ),
956:     .splitter_bridge_rdy    ( buf_ariane_rst_noc3_ready_i ),
957:     //axi lite signals
958:     //write address channel
959:     .m_axi_awaddr           ( rst_axi_req.aw.addr         ),
960:     .m_axi_awvalid          ( rst_axi_req.aw_valid        ),
961:     .m_axi_awready          ( rst_axi_resp.aw_ready       ),
962:     //write data channel
963:     .m_axi_wdata            ( rst_axi_req.w.data          ),
964:     .m_axi_wstrb            ( rst_axi_req.w.strb          ),
965:     .m_axi_wvalid           ( rst_axi_req.w_valid         ),
966:     .m_axi_wready           ( rst_axi_resp.w_ready        ),
967:     //read address channel
968:     .m_axi_araddr           ( rst_axi_req.ar.addr         ),
969:     .m_axi_arvalid          ( rst_axi_req.ar_valid        ),
970:     .m_axi_arready          ( rst_axi_resp.ar_ready       ),
971:     //read data channel
972:     .m_axi_rdata            ( rst_axi_resp.r.data         ),
973:     .m_axi_rresp            ( rst_axi_resp.r.resp         ),
974:     .m_axi_rvalid           ( rst_axi_resp.r_valid        ),
975:     .m_axi_rready           ( rst_axi_req.r_ready         ),
976:     //write response channel
977:     .m_axi_bresp            ( rst_axi_resp.b.resp         ),
978:     .m_axi_bvalid           ( rst_axi_resp.b_valid        ),
979:     .m_axi_bready           ( rst_axi_req.b_ready         ),
980:     // non-axi-lite signals
981:     .w_reqbuf_size          (                               ),
982:     .r_reqbuf_size          (                               )
983:   );
984: 
985:   // tie off signals not used by AXI-lite
986:   assign rst_axi_req.aw.id     = '0;
987:   assign rst_axi_req.aw.len    = '0;
988:   assign rst_axi_req.aw.size   = 3'b11;// 8byte
989:   assign rst_axi_req.aw.burst  = '0;
990:   assign rst_axi_req.aw.lock   = '0;
991:   assign rst_axi_req.aw.cache  = '0;
992:   assign rst_axi_req.aw.prot   = '0;
993:   assign rst_axi_req.aw.qos    = '0;
994:   assign rst_axi_req.aw.region = '0;
995:   assign rst_axi_req.aw.atop   = '0;
996:   assign rst_axi_req.w.last    = 1'b1;
997:   assign rst_axi_req.ar.id     = '0;
998:   assign rst_axi_req.ar.len    = '0;
999:   assign rst_axi_req.ar.size   = 3'b11;// 8byte
1000:   assign rst_axi_req.ar.burst  = '0

411:       .data_i     ( dm_slave_rdata            )

1788:   assign acct_axi_req.aw.atop   = '0;
1789:   assign acct_axi_req.w.last    = 1'b1;
1790:   assign acct_axi_req.ar.id     = '0;

1792:   assign acct_axi_req.ar.size   = 3'b11;// 8byte
1793:   assign acct_axi_req.ar.burst  = '0;
1794:   assign acct_axi_req.ar.lock   = '0;
1795:   assign acct_axi_req.ar.cache  = '0;
1796:   assign acct_axi_req.ar.prot   = '0;
1797:   assign acct_axi_req.ar.qos    = '0;

1: // Description: Wrapper for the PKT.
2: //
3: 
4: 
5: module pkt_wrapper #(
6:     parameter int unsigned AXI_ADDR_WIDTH = 64,
7:     parameter int unsigned AXI_DATA_WIDTH = 64,
8:     parameter int unsigned AXI_ID_WIDTH   = 10,
9:     parameter FUSE_MEM_SIZE = 34
10: )(
11:            clk_i,
12:            rst_ni,
13:            reglk_ctrl_i,
14:            acct_ctrl_i,
15:            fuse_req_o,
16:            fuse_addr_o,
17:            fuse_rdata_i,
18:            axi_req_i, 
19:            axi_resp_o,
20:            rst_5
21:        );
22: 
23: 
24:     input  logic                   clk_i;
25:     input  logic                   rst_ni;
26:     input logic [7 :0]             reglk_ctrl_i; // register lock values
27:     input logic                    acct_ctrl_i;
28:     input  ariane_axi::req_t       axi_req_i;
29:     output ariane_axi::resp_t      axi_resp_o;
30:     output logic                   fuse_req_o;
31:     output logic [31:0]            fuse_addr_o;
32:     input  logic [31:0]            fuse_rdata_i;
33: 
34:     input logic rst_5;
35: 
36: // internal signals
37: 
38: wire [63:0] pkey_loc; 
39: 
40: // signals from AXI 4 Lite
41: logic [AXI_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] address;
42: logic                      en, en_acct;
43: logic                      we;
44: logic [63:0] wdata;
45: logic [63:0] rdata;
46: 
47: 
48: ///////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////
49:     // -----------------------------
50:     // AXI Interface Logic
51:     // -----------------------------
52:     axi_lite_interface #(
53:         .AXI_ADDR_WIDTH ( AXI_ADDR_WIDTH ),
54:         .AXI_DATA_WIDTH ( AXI_DATA_WIDTH ),
55:         .AXI_ID_WIDTH   ( AXI_ID_WIDTH    )
56:     ) axi_lite_interface_i (
57:         .clk_i      ( clk_i      ),
58:         .rst_ni     ( rst_ni     ),
59:         .axi_req_i  ( axi_req_i  ),
60:         .axi_resp_o ( axi_resp_o ),
61:         .address_o  ( address    ),
62:         .en_o       ( en_acct    ),
63:         .we_o       ( we         ),
64:         .data_i     ( rdata      ),
65:         .data_o     ( wdata      )
66:     );
67: 
68:     assign en = en_acct && acct_ctrl_i; 
69: 
70: ///////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////
71: // Implement APB I/O map to PKT interface
72: // Write side
73: always @(posedge clk_i)
74:     begin
75:         if(~(rst_ni && ~rst_5))
76:             begin
77:                 fuse_req_o <= 0;
78:                 fuse_addr_o <= 0;
79:             end
80:         else if(en && we)
81:             case(address[7:3])
82:                 0:
83:                     fuse_req_o  <= wdata[0];
84:                 1:
85:                     fuse_addr_o <= wdata;
86:                 default:
87:                     ;
88:             endcase
89:     end // always @ (posedge wb_clk_i)
90: 
91: //// Read side
92: always @(*)
93:     begin
94:       rdata = 64'b0; 
95:       if (en) begin
96:         rdata = fuse_rdata_i; 
97:         case(address[7:3])
98:             0:
99:                 rdata = {31'b0, fuse_req_o};
100:             1:
101:                 rdata = fuse_addr_o;
102:             2:
103:                 rdata = reglk_ctrl_i[4] ? 'b0 : pkey_loc[63:32];
104:             3:
105:                 rdata = reglk_ctrl_i[5] ? 'b0 : pkey_loc[31:0];
106:             4:
107:                 rdata = reglk_ctrl_i[6] ? 'b0 : fuse_rdata_i;
108:             default:
109:                 if (fuse_addr_o <= 110)
110:                     rdata = 32'b0;
111:         endcase
112:       end // if
113:     end // always @ (*)
114: 
115: pkt # (
116:         .FUSE_MEM_SIZE(FUSE_MEM_SIZE)
117:     ) i_pkt(
118:             .clk_i(clk_i),
119:             .rst_ni(rst_ni && ~rst_5),
120:             .req_i(1'b1),
121:             .fuse_indx_i(fuse_addr_o),
122:             .pkey_loc_o(pkey_loc)
123:         );
124: 
125: endmodule

1:  /*
2:  * Peripheral Key Table (PKT) takes the fuse mem index and returns the actual destination address
3:  * for that FUSE data
4:  */
5: 
6: 
7: 
8: module pkt (
9:    input  logic         clk_i,
10:    input  logic         rst_ni,
11:    input  logic         req_i,
12:    input  logic [31:0]  fuse_indx_i, // peripheral index of the key
13:    output logic [63:0]  pkey_loc_o // peripheral key location in ROM2 as the output
14: );
15: 
16:     parameter FUSE_MEM_SIZE = 100;
17: 
18: // Store dest address values here. 
19:     const logic [FUSE_MEM_SIZE-1:0][63:0] fuse_indx_mem = {
20:         //// JTAG (only a place holder, JTAG hash is not mapped to AXI mem)
21:         //ariane_soc::JTAGHASH,
22:         //RNG poly
23:         ariane_soc::RNGPoly128_3,
24:         ariane_soc::RNGPoly128_2,
25:         ariane_soc::RNGPoly128_1,
26:         ariane_soc::RNGPoly128_0,
27:         ariane_soc::RNGPoly64_1,
28:         ariane_soc::RNGPoly64_0,
29:         ariane_soc::RNGPoly32_0,
30:         ariane_soc::RNGPoly16_0,
31:         //HMAC okey hash
32:         ariane_soc::HMACoKeyHash_7, 
33:         ariane_soc::HMACoKeyHash_6, 
34:         ariane_soc::HMACoKeyHash_5, 
35:         ariane_soc::HMACoKeyHash_4, 
36:         ariane_soc::HMACoKeyHash_3, 
37:         ariane_soc::HMACoKeyHash_2, 
38:         ariane_soc::HMACoKeyHash_1, 
39:         ariane_soc::HMACoKeyHash_0, 
40:         //HMAC ikey hash
41:         ariane_soc::HMACiKeyHash_7, 
42:         ariane_soc::HMACiKeyHash_6, 
43:         ariane_soc::HMACiKeyHash_5, 
44:         ariane_soc::HMACiKeyHash_4, 
45:         ariane_soc::HMACiKeyHash_3, 
46:         ariane_soc::HMACiKeyHash_2, 
47:         ariane_soc::HMACiKeyHash_1, 
48:         ariane_soc::HMACiKeyHash_0, 
49:         //HMAC key 
50:         ariane_soc::HMACKey_7, 
51:         ariane_soc::HMACKey_6, 
52:         ariane_soc::HMACKey_5, 
53:         ariane_soc::HMACKey_4, 
54:         ariane_soc::HMACKey_3, 
55:         ariane_soc::HMACKey_2, 
56:         ariane_soc::HMACKey_1, 
57:         ariane_soc::HMACKey_0, 
58:         // Access control for master 2. First 4 bits for peripheral 0, next 4 for p1 and so on.
59:         ariane_soc::ACCT_M_22, 
60:         ariane_soc::ACCT_M_21, 
61:         ariane_soc::ACCT_M_20, 
62:         // Access control for master 1. First 4 bits for peripheral 0, next 4 for p1 and so on.
63:         ariane_soc::ACCT_M_12, 
64:         ariane_soc::ACCT_M_11, 
65:         ariane_soc::ACCT_M_10, 
66:         // Access control for master 0. First 4 bits for peripheral 0, next 4 for p1 and so on.
67:         ariane_soc::ACCT_M_02, 
68:         ariane_soc::ACCT_M_01, 
69:         ariane_soc::ACCT_M_00, 
70:         // SHA Key
71:         ariane_soc::SHA256Key_5,
72:         ariane_soc::SHA256Key_4,
73:         ariane_soc::SHA256Key_3,
74:         ariane_soc::SHA256Key_2,
75:         ariane_soc::SHA256Key_1,
76:         ariane_soc::SHA256Key_0,
77:         // AES2 Key 2
78:         ariane_soc::AES2Key2_3,
79:         ariane_soc::AES2Key2_2,
80:         ariane_soc::AES2Key2_1,
81:         ariane_soc::AES2Key2_0,   // address for LSB 32 bits
82:         // AES2 Key 1
83:         ariane_soc::AES2Key1_3,
84:         ariane_soc::AES2Key1_2,
85:         ariane_soc::AES2Key1_1,
86:         ariane_soc::AES2Key1_0,   // address for LSB 32 bits
87:         // AES2 Key 0
88:         ariane_soc::AES2Key0_3,
89:         ariane_soc::AES2Key0_2,
90:         ariane_soc::AES2Key0_1,
91:         ariane_soc::AES2Key0_0,   // address for LSB 32 bits
92:         // AES1 Key 2
93:         ariane_soc::AES1Key2_7,
94:         ariane_soc::AES1Key2_6,
95:         ariane_soc::AES1Key2_5,
96:         ariane_soc::AES1Key2_4,
97:         ariane_soc::AES1Key2_3,
98:         ariane_soc::AES1Key2_2,
99:         ariane_soc::AES1Key2_1,
100:         ariane_soc::AES1Key2_0,   // address for LSB 32 bits
101:         // AES1 Key 1
102:         ariane_soc::AES1Key1_7,
103:         ariane_soc::AES1Key1_6,
104:         ariane_soc::AES1Ke

10: //
11: // Author: Florian Zaruba, ETH Zurich
12: // Date: 17/07/2017
13: // Description: AXI Lite compatible interface
14: //
15: 
16: module axi_lite_interface #(
17:     parameter int unsigned AXI_ADDR_WIDTH = 64,
18:     parameter int unsigned AXI_DATA_WIDTH = 64,
19:     parameter int unsigned AXI_ID_WIDTH   = 10
20: ) (
21:     input logic                       clk_i,    // Clock
22:     input logic                       rst_ni,  // Asynchronous reset active low
23: 
24:     input  ariane_axi::req_t          axi_req_i,
25:     output ariane_axi::resp_t         axi_resp_o,
26: 
27:     output logic [AXI_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] address_o,
28:     output logic                      en_o,        // transaction is valid
29:     output logic                      we_o,        // write
30:     input  logic [AXI_DATA_WIDTH-1:0] data_i,      // data
31:     output logic [AXI_DATA_WIDTH-1:0] data_o
32: );
33: 
34:     // The RLAST signal is not required, and is considered asserted for every transfer on the read data channel.
35:     enum logic [1:0] { IDLE, READ, WRITE, WRITE_B } state_q, state_d;
36:     // save the trans id, we will need it for reflection otherwise we are not plug compatible to the AXI standard
37:     logic [AXI_ID_WIDTH-1:0]   trans_id_n, trans_id_q;
38:     // address register
39:     logic [AXI_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] address_n,  address_q;
40: 
41:     // pass through read data on the read data channel
42:     assign axi_resp_o.r.data = data_i;
43:     // send back the transaction id we've latched
44:     assign axi_resp_o.r.id = trans_id_q;
45:     assign axi_resp_o.b.id = trans_id_q;
46:     // set r_last to one as defined by the AXI4 - Lite standard
47:     assign axi_resp_o.r.last = 1'b1;
48:     // we do not support any errors so set response flag to all zeros
49:     assign axi_resp_o.b.resp = 2'b0;
50:     assign axi_resp_o.r.resp = 2'b0;
51:     // output data which we want to write to the slave
52:     assign data_o = axi_req_i.w.data;
53:     // ------------------------
54:     // AXI4-Lite State Machine
55:     // ------------------------
56:     always_comb begin
57:         // default signal assignment
58:         state_d    = state_q;
59:         address_n  = address_q;
60:         trans_id_n = trans_id_q;
61: 
62:         // we'll answer a write request only if we got address and data
63:         axi_resp_o.aw_ready = 1'b0;
64:         axi_resp_o.w_ready  = 1'b0;
65:         axi_resp_o.b_valid  = 1'b0;
66: 
67:         axi_resp_o.ar_ready = 1'b0;
68:         axi_resp_o.r_valid  = 1'b0;
69: 
70:         address_o      = '0;
71:         we_o           = 1'b0;
72:         en_o           = 1'b0;
73: 
74:         case (state_q)
75:             // we are ready to accept a new request
76:             IDLE: begin
77:                 // we've git a valid write request, we also know that we have asserted the aw_ready
78:                 if (axi_req_i.aw_valid) begin
79:                     axi_resp_o.aw_ready = 1'b1;
80:                     // this costs performance but the interconnect does not obey the AXI standard
81:                     // e.g.: we could wait for aw_valid && w_valid to do the transaction.
82:                     state_d = WRITE;
83:                     // save address
84:                     address_n = axi_req_i.aw
[TRUNCATED]

1891:   /////////////////////////////
1892:   // DMA

1894: 
1895:   ariane_axi::req_t    dma_axi_req;
1896:   ariane_axi::resp_t   dma_axi_resp;
1897: 
1898:   dma_wrapper #(
1899:     .AXI_ADDR_WIDTH ( AxiAddrWidth ),
1900:     .AXI_DATA_WIDTH ( AxiDataWidth ),
1901:     .AXI_ID_WIDTH   ( AxiIdWidth   )
1902:   ) i_dma_wrapper (

1905:     .reglk_ctrl_i  ( reglk_ctrl[8*8+7:8*8]),
1906:     .acct_ctrl_i   ( acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][8]),
1907:     .pmpcfg_i      ( pmpcfg_i  ),
1908:     .pmpaddr_i     ( pmpaddr_i  ),
1909:     .we_flag       ( we_flag_3 ),
1910:     .axi_req_i     ( dma_axi_req  ),
1911:     .axi_resp_o    ( dma_axi_resp ),
1912:     .rst_8
1913:   );
1914: 
1915:   noc_axilite_bridge #(
1916:     .SLAVE_RESP_BYTEWIDTH   ( 8             ),
1917:     .SWAP_ENDIANESS         ( SwapEndianess )
1918:   ) i_dma_axilite_bridge (
1919:     .clk                    ( clk_i                         ),
1920:     .rst                    ( ~rst_ni                       ),
1921:     // to/from NOC
1922:     .splitter_bridge_val    ( buf_ariane_dma_noc2_valid_i ),
1923:     .splitter_bridge_data   ( buf_ariane_dma_noc2_data_i  ),
1924:     .bridge_splitter_rdy    ( ariane_dma_buf_noc2_ready_o ),
1925:     .bridge_splitter_val    ( ariane_dma_buf_noc3_valid_o ),
1926:     .bridge_splitter_data   ( ariane_dma_buf_noc3_data_o  ),
1927:     .splitter_bridge_rdy    ( buf_ariane_dma_noc3_ready_i ),
1928:     //axi lite signals
1929:     //write address channel
1930:     .m_axi_awaddr           ( dma_axi_req.aw.addr         ),
1931:     .m_axi_awvalid          ( dma_axi_req.aw_valid        ),
1932:     .m_axi_awready          ( dma_axi_resp.aw_ready       ),
1933:     //write data channel
1934:     .m_axi_wdata            ( dma_axi_req.w.data          ),
1935:     .m_axi_wstrb            ( dma_axi_req.w.strb          ),
1936:     .m_axi_wvalid           ( dma_axi_req.w_valid         ),
1937:     .m_axi_wready           ( dma_axi_resp.w_ready        ),
1938:     //read address channel
1939:     .m_axi_araddr           ( dma_axi_req.ar.addr         ),
1940:     .m_axi_arvalid          ( dma_axi_req.ar_valid        ),
1941:     .m_axi_arready          ( dma_axi_resp.ar_ready       ),
1942:     //read data channel
1943:     .m_axi_rdata            ( dma_axi_resp.r.data         ),
1944:     .m_axi_rresp            ( dma_axi_resp.r.resp         ),
1945:     .m_axi_rvalid           ( dma_axi_resp.r_valid        ),
1946:     .m_axi_rready           ( dma_axi_req.r_ready         ),
1947:     //write response channel
1948:     .m_axi_bresp            ( dma_axi_resp.b.resp         ),
1949:     .m_axi_bvalid           ( dma_axi_resp.b_valid        ),
1950:     .m_axi_bready           ( dma_axi_req.b_ready         ),
1951:     // non-axi-lite signals
1952:     .w_reqbuf_size          (                               ),
1953:     .r_reqbuf_size          (                               )
1954:   );
1955: 
1956:   // tie off signals not used by AXI-lite
1957:   assign dma_axi_req.aw.id     = '0;
1958:   assign dma_axi_req.aw.len    = '0;
1959:   assign dma_axi_req.aw.size   = 3'b11;// 8byte
1960:   assign dma_axi_req.aw.burst  = '0;
1961:   assign dma_axi_req.aw.lock   = '0;
1962:   assign dma_axi_req.aw.cache  = '0;
1963:   assign dma_axi_req.aw.prot   = '0;
1964:   assign dma_axi_req.aw.qos    = '0;
1965:   assign dma_axi_req.aw.region = '0;
1966:   assign dma_axi_req.aw.atop   = '0;
1967:   assign dma_axi_req.w.last    = 1'b1;
1968:   assign dma_axi_req.ar.id     = '0;
